skill.name: seo-keyword-strategist
skill.description: Analyzes keyword usage in provided content, calculates density, suggests semantic variations, and flags over-optimization risks.
skill.summary: Reviews content for keyword density, semantic coverage, and natural SEO optimization opportunities.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found no malicious behavior or executable payloads in the single skill file. The static backtick, weak crypto, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives caused by Markdown formatting and SEO guidance text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Resource Reference
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. Line 25 uses inline Markdown around a relative documentation path, not a shell command or Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is a plain instruction to open a Markdown resource path. No command syntax, interpreter call, or user input execution is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Fenced Output Example
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. Lines 69-74 are a Markdown output template showing SEO fields, not executable shell or Ruby content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fenced block contains labels such as Primary, Secondary, LSI Keywords, and Entities. It provides display format only.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. Line 3 is the skill description for keyword analysis and contains no cryptographic algorithm or hashing instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line describes SEO keyword usage analysis. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. Line 43 advises avoiding keyword stuffing and does not request host, user, network, or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is SEO content guidance. I found no evidence of reconnaissance commands or collection of system information.

content.value_statement: Keyword-heavy pages can miss search intent or trigger over-optimization. This skill reviews content and proposes natural keyword, entity, and semantic improvements.
actual_capabilities: Calculates primary and secondary keyword density from provided content.
Suggests semantic variations and related LSI keyword ideas.
Identifies entities and concepts that can strengthen topical relevance.
Flags likely keyword stuffing and unnatural repetition.
Creates keyword placement recommendations for headings, body copy, and metadata.
Produces a practical checklist for revising SEO content.
limitations: It does not fetch live search volume, rankings, or competitor data.
It depends on the accuracy and completeness of the content provided.
It cannot guarantee ranking improvements or search engine outcomes.
It may need human review for regulated, medical, legal, or financial topics.

use_cases.title: Refresh Existing Blog Content
use_cases.description: Review an article for keyword balance, missing entities, and natural semantic coverage before publishing updates.

use_cases.title: Plan Product Page Keywords
use_cases.description: Map primary, secondary, and related terms for a product page without forcing repetitive copy.

use_cases.title: Build Topic Cluster Briefs
use_cases.description: Generate keyword groups, related questions, and entity targets for a new cluster of supporting pages.

prompt_templates.title: Check Keyword Density
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this draft for the primary keyword [keyword]. Calculate density, list repeated phrases, and suggest natural edits.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Semantic Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this content for [topic]. Suggest semantic variations, related entities, and missing subtopics that support search intent.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Keyword Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a page brief.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a keyword strategy for a [page type] targeting [audience]. Include primary, secondary, LSI, entities, and placement guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Over-Optimization Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this page for keyword stuffing and unnatural repetition. Recommend specific rewrites that preserve intent and improve readability.

output_examples.input: A service page draft targeting 'managed IT support'.
output_examples.output: The skill reports density, secondary terms, related entities, placement gaps, and a revision checklist.

output_examples.input: A blog post about email deliverability for SaaS teams.
output_examples.output: The skill suggests semantic terms, question-based keywords, entity coverage, and overuse warnings.

output_examples.input: A product category page with repeated purchase keywords.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies repetition, recommends natural variants, and proposes headings that match search intent.

best_practices: Provide the full draft, target keyword, audience, and page type.
Use recommendations to improve clarity before adding more keywords.
Validate final keyword choices with current search data when available.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a replacement for live keyword research tools.
Do not add every suggested term if it weakens readability.
Do not optimize sensitive advice without qualified human review.

faq.question: Does this skill perform live keyword research?
faq.answer: No. It analyzes provided content and suggests improvements based on the topic and draft.

faq.question: Can it calculate exact keyword density?
faq.answer: Yes, when the target keyword and content are provided in the prompt.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it prevent keyword stuffing?
faq.answer: It can flag likely overuse and suggest more natural wording.

faq.question: Is it useful for topic clusters?
faq.answer: Yes. It can group related terms, entities, and questions for supporting pages.

faq.question: Does it guarantee better rankings?
faq.answer: No. Search performance depends on competition, technical SEO, authority, and content quality.
